sections.name: Asura, Pyrkaion
Philosophical Zeal

link.alt: Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea pg. 80
link.name: Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea pg. 80

sources.name: Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea

sections.text: With each philosophical victory, these asuras increasingly wrestle with their own thoughts, recognizing that they're as vulnerable to selfdelusion as their victims. This can drive a pyrkaion to ravenous fits of reading and research before jealously sequestering the texts. Sometimes a pyrkaion with an inflated ego instead seeks and loses debates, craving the sting of humility to recenter their thoughts.

link.alt: Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea pg. 80
link.name: Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea pg. 80

sources.name: Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea

sections.text: Reality is imperfect, yet many mortals focus on their world's virtues while refusing to address its failures. A pyrkaion believes mortal delusion is the last wall propping up a broken universe, and wisdom is the pickaxe that will tear it down. Pyrkaions vacillate between teaching mortals enough to wither from indulgent self-awareness and shredding their students' institutions through insidious interrogation that leads to infighting as the asura watches.

link.alt: Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea pg. 80

family.name: Asura

sections.name: Asura Ranas
sections.text: The mightiest asuras are demigods in their own right, and despite knowing their divine power is forged from a corrupted lie, they do not eschew worship. Gaining the might of divinity offers the power to unmake reality, and the adoration of followers allows asura ranas to reveal the faults of existence to mortals.

sections.name: Asura Realms
sections.text: Asuras command vast empires in the corners of Hell. While their realms are tiny compared to the massive hierarchy of Asmodeus and devilkind, they exist in a tacit agreement of coexistence with their diabolic neighbors

sections.name: Asura Recruits
sections.text: Many asuras were formerly rakshasas or yakshas, swayed to the asuras' cause and transformed as a result of their broken vows or failed duties. Residents of the Outer Planes are far less likely to be swayed but can sometimes become asuras as well— celestials when they lose faith in the system they once supported and fiends drawn in by the promise that they were judged, damned, and condemned by a flawed system.

sections.name: Geryon
sections.text: A former asura rana, Geryon joined the ranks of Hell, betraying his asura brethren to prove his loyalty to Asmodeus. Yet some believe that this was simply a ploy for Geryon to draw closer to true divinity, to gain the power necessary to unravel reality— including all of Hell's works.

family.text:
Asuras claim to recall a time before anything existed, a time of perfect simplicity, peace, and innocence. The act of creation was also one of unfathomable tragedy, as it brought forth a corrupted multiverse filled with suffering and broken promises. As such, asura are unmoving bastions of an uncomfortable truth: existence is flawed, irredeemably cruel, and must be destroyed. While other creatures ignorantly accept the reality that has been thrust upon them or seek mastery over their imperfect circumstances, asuras work to undo this horrific injustice. They reject all consolation, happiness, pride, pleasure, and love in their mission, for these things are born from what they seek to unmake. Even power is only useful as a tool to bring about the dissolution of everyone and everything.  
  
 Despite their overarching goal, asuras aren't necessarily belligerent toward any denizens of the mortal Universe they encounter. They're not nihilistic, though many mistake them as such—asuras believe strongly in virtue, as it's virtue that drives them forward in their terrible task. Some will perform righteous deeds even as they plan to ultimately obliterate the beneficiaries of their charity, and many mortals consider them divine protectors worthy of worship.

traits.name: Uncommon
traits.text: Something of uncommon rarity requires special training or comes from a particular culture or part of the world. Some character choices give access to uncommon options, and the GM can choose to allow access for anyone. Less is known about uncommon creatures than common creatures. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creature is increased by 2.

traits.name: Asura
traits.text: asura These fiends are physical manifestations of divine accidents. Asuras typically have darkvision, immunity to curses, and a weakness to holy.

traits.name: Spirit
traits.text: Effects with this trait can affect creatures with spiritual essence and might deal spirit damage. A creature with this trait is defined by its spiritual essence. Spirit creatures often lack a material form.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

automatic_abilities.name: Aura of Sophistry
automatic_abilities.text: The pyrkaion hones others' thoughts, enabling extraordinary reasoning or ruinous revelation. If the pyrkaion has the holy trait (see Glorious Visage below), all allied creatures within the aura gain a +1 status bonus to check to Recall Knowledge and resistance 4 to mental damage. If the pyrkaion has the unholy trait, all unallied creatures in the area take a –1 penalty to checks to Recall Knowledge and gain the effects of the Dubious Knowledge feat when Recalling Knowledge; whenever an affected creature Recalls Knowledge, they become stupefied 1 and gain weakness 4 to mental damage for 1d4 rounds.

traits.name: Aura
traits.text: An aura is an emanation that continually ebbs out from you, affecting creatures within a certain radius. Aura can also refer to the magical signature of an item.

traits.name: Divine
traits.text:
This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to divine magic.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

automatic_abilities.effect: The pyrkaion attempts to counteract the effect (counteract rank 4, counteract modifier +16). If successful, the pyrkaion can choose to corrupt the effect, dealing 2d6 persistent mental damage to the triggering effect's targets unless those targets succeed at a DC 26 Will save.
automatic_abilities.name: Witty Reversal

traits.name: Linguistic
traits.text: An effect with this trait depends on language comprehension. A linguistic effect that targets a creature works only if the target understands the language you are using.

automatic_abilities.trigger: A creature within 30 feet uses a linguistic or mental effect

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

traits.name: Range
traits.text: These attacks will either list a finite range or a range increment, which follows the normal rules for range increments.

ability.name: Glorious Visage
ability.text: The asura sanctifies themselves as either holy or unholy, gaining the trait corresponding to their choice and losing the opposing trait; their strikes, spells, and abilities also gain the trait corresponding to their choice. The asura also gains weakness 8 to the opposing sanctification and loses any weakness to its chosen sanctification. The choice is permanent until the asura uses this ability to change their sanctification.

ability.critical_failure: As failure, but until the end of their next turn, the creature also takes 2d6 mental damage when Recalling Knowledge.
ability.critical_success: The creature becomes immune for 1 hour.
ability.failure: If the creature doesn't Recall Knowledge by the end of its next turn, it becomes confused for 1 round.
ability.name: Insidious Query
ability.text: The pyrkaion questions a creature within 40 feet, who must attempt a DC 26 Will save.

traits.name: Auditory
traits.text: Auditory actions and effects rely on sound. An action with the auditory trait can be successfully performed only if the creature using the action can speak or otherwise produce the required sounds. A spell or effect with the auditory trait has its effect only if the target can hear it. This applies only to sound-based parts of the effect, as determined by the GM. This is different from a sonic effect, which still affects targets who can’t hear it (such as deaf targets) as long as the effect itself makes sound.

traits.name: Linguistic
traits.text: An effect with this trait depends on language comprehension. A linguistic effect that targets a creature works only if the target understands the language you are using.

universal_monster_ability.name: Darkvision
universal_monster_ability.text: A monster with darkvision can see perfectly well in areas of darkness and dim light, though such vision is in black and white only. Some forms of magical darkness, such as a 4th-level *darkness* spell, block normal darkvision. A monster with greater darkvision, however, can see through even these forms of magical darkness.
